# Pulsys

> Pulsys is an open-source, authenticated pull-through cache for the Hugging Face Hub.

Pulsys stores model artifacts on local disk and serves subsequent requests from
the cache. Existing Hugging Face clients connect through `HF_ENDPOINT`.
Upstream cache misses require a read-only Hugging Face token; clients
authenticate to Pulsys with Pulsys API keys. The supported deployment is a
single proxy replica backed by persistent disk and a Postgres admin plane.

## Start here

- [Project website](https://pulsys.io/): Product overview, measured performance, and quick start.
- [README](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/pulsys-io/pulsys/main/README.md): Local setup, Kubernetes deployment, supported scope, and project status.
- [Development guide](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/pulsys-io/pulsys/main/DEVELOPMENT.md): Build, run, test, and navigate the repository.

## Operations

- [Architecture](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/pulsys-io/pulsys/main/docs/architecture.md): Components, request flow, cache warming, storage, and deployment topology.
- [Security](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/pulsys-io/pulsys/main/docs/security.md): Credential model, threat model, parser hardening, deployment hardening, and supply-chain posture.
- [OIDC and SSO](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/pulsys-io/pulsys/main/docs/oidc.md): Keycloak, Amazon Cognito, and AWS IAM Identity Center setup.
- [Benchmarks](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/pulsys-io/pulsys/main/docs/benchmarks.md): Methodology, results, limitations, and reproduction steps.
- [Internals](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/pulsys-io/pulsys/main/docs/internals.md): io_uring, sendfile, Hugging Face protocol handling, and operating-system tuning.

## Deployment and components

- [Helm chart](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/pulsys-io/pulsys/main/deploy/charts/pulsys/README.md): Kubernetes values, examples, and production configuration.
- [Admin console](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/pulsys-io/pulsys/main/admin-ui/README.md): Next.js administration interface.
- [AWS benchmark infrastructure](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/pulsys-io/pulsys/main/infra/cdk/README.md): Packer, CDK, and SSM benchmark harness.
- [Configuration source](https://github.com/pulsys-io/pulsys/blob/main/internal/config/config.go): Runtime flags and their `PULSYS_` environment variables.

## Project

- [Roadmap](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/pulsys-io/pulsys/main/ROADMAP.md): Planned work and explicit non-goals.
- [Contributing](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/pulsys-io/pulsys/main/CONTRIBUTING.md): Development workflow, DCO, testing, and review requirements.
- [Security policy](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/pulsys-io/pulsys/main/SECURITY.md): Supported versions and vulnerability reporting.
- [License](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/pulsys-io/pulsys/main/LICENSE): Apache License 2.0.

## Optional

- [The Price of a Model Pull](https://pulsys.io/blog/price-of-a-model-pull/): Benchmarking repeated model downloads and the warm cache path.
- [Chasing the Absolute Floor](https://pulsys.io/blog/darwin-warm-path-optimizations/): Darwin sendfile and single-syscall warm-path engineering.
- [Code of Conduct](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/pulsys-io/pulsys/main/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md): Community standards.
